skill.name: skill-forge
skill.description: Skill Forge helps create and improve AI assistant skills through intent analysis, structured workflow design, prompt engineering guidance, validation, and packaging support.
skill.summary: Design, validate, and package well-structured AI assistant skills.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical malicious-combination heuristic, but review found no prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or confirmed malicious intent. The meaningful risks are publishable with warnings: bundled Python scripts read user-supplied skill paths and create archives, while documentation recommends external commands and writes to the Claude skills directory.

medium_findings.title: Archive Packaging Reads User-Supplied Directories
medium_findings.description: The packaging helper accepts a skill path, walks files recursively, and writes them into a zip archive. This is legitimate for skill distribution, but users must avoid broad paths because non-hidden private files could be included.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file traversal and archive write are direct code behavior. Confidence is high for the capability, but medium for exploitability because execution requires an explicit user-provided path.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Recommends External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The documentation includes shell commands for pip, npx, Python validation, packaging, copy, unzip, and GraphViz installation. These commands are expected for this development workflow, but they introduce dependency and supply-chain risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external commands are explicitly shown in documentation and include npx execution. They appear intentional and workflow-related, not covert or malicious.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Claude Skills Directory Access
medium_findings.description: Several installation examples target ~/.claude/skills and show copying or unzipping skill packages there. This is normal for Claude skill installation, but it writes into a hidden home-directory configuration area.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden-directory references are direct and repeated. The usage is legitimate installation guidance, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Network and API-Key Examples Are Boilerplate
low_findings.description: SKILL-ENHANCED.md contains a generic requests-based API client example with a bearer token and example.com base URL. This is not active credential exfiltration, but users should review generated API examples before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern contains API key handling and HTTP code, but it appears inside instructional boilerplate with an example.com URL. No evidence found that the bundled scripts transmit secrets.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many weak-cryptography hits in Markdown and diagram files, but targeted review did not find operational weak cryptographic code. These appear to be keyword or Markdown false positives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are frontmatter, argparse descriptions, or validation text rather than cryptographic operations. Confidence is low because the reported category is not supported by reviewed code context.

low_findings.title: Critical Malicious-Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner combined command, filesystem, network, and credential indicators into a critical heuristic. Review found these indicators split across documentation, packaging utilities, and boilerplate API examples with no evidence of coordinated exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The underlying indicators exist, but semantic review separates them into legitimate contexts. No evidence found of hidden network calls, prompt injection, or secret theft.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Archive Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The package script recursively adds files from the supplied skill path into a zip archive. Review packaged contents before sharing archives.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is explicit. It is useful but can expose files if pointed at the wrong directory.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Package Execution Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation demonstrates npx claude-flow@alpha execution. Running remote packages can execute code fetched from the package ecosystem.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx command is direct evidence of remote package execution guidance. It is documented user-facing guidance, not silent execution by the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Home Directory Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: Installation examples write skill packages into ~/.claude/skills. This is expected for skill installation but should remain visible to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden directory writes are explicit. They are legitimate for installation, so the risk is limited to user awareness and path review.

content.value_statement: Creating reusable AI skills is difficult when intent, structure, resources, and validation are unclear. Skill Forge provides a systematic process for designing, refining, validating, and packaging assistant skills.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through intent analysis before writing a skill.
Defines a seven-phase process for skill design, resource planning, instruction writing, validation, and packaging.
Explains how to structure SKILL.md, references, scripts, assets, and GraphViz process diagrams.
Provides Python helper scripts for validating skill structure and packaging a skill directory into a zip archive.
Includes guidance for improving existing skills through diagnostics, refactoring, testing, and iteration.
Documents prompt engineering patterns such as self-consistency, plan-and-solve structure, and negative examples.
limitations: The skill does not automatically prove that a generated skill is safe or effective.
The bundled validator uses simple heuristics and can miss semantic quality issues.
The packaging script can archive broad directory contents if the user provides a broad path.
Several command examples require local tools such as Python, pip, GraphViz, or npx.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated workflow into a structured AI assistant skill with clear triggers, steps, resources, and validation criteria.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Diagnose a skill that activates poorly, has unclear instructions, or needs better resource organization.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Delivery
use_cases.description: Use validation and packaging helpers to prepare skills for internal distribution with consistent structure.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new reusable skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Skill Forge to help me create a skill for [workflow]. Ask only the questions needed to define the goal, users, inputs, outputs, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Skill Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request to improve an existing skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Skill Forge to review this existing skill structure. Identify unclear triggers, missing resources, weak instructions, and validation gaps before suggesting edits.

prompt_templates.title: Add Resources and Validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a skill with scripts or references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Skill Forge to design the resource plan for this skill. Decide what belongs in SKILL.md, references, scripts, assets, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Distribution
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before sharing a skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Skill Forge to perform a final readiness review. Check metadata, progressive disclosure, bundled resources, validation output, packaging steps, and known risks.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for monthly release note generation.
output: A clear skill purpose and activation description.
A recommended SKILL.md outline with workflow phases.
A resource plan for templates, examples, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: Improve a skill that is too long and hard to maintain.
output: A diagnosis of duplicated instructions and overloaded sections.
A progressive disclosure plan that moves detailed material into references.
A focused validation checklist for the revised skill.

output_examples.input: Package a completed skill for distribution.
output: A validation-first workflow for checking metadata and resource references.
A packaging command using the bundled helper script.
A short risk note about reviewing archived file contents before sharing.

best_practices: Start with concrete use cases before writing skill instructions.
Keep SKILL.md focused and move detailed reference material into separate files.
Run validation and manually review packaged contents before distribution.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad skills with vague triggers and no concrete workflow.
Do not include large reference dumps in SKILL.md when targeted resources would work better.
Do not package directories before checking that private or unrelated files are excluded.

faq.question: What does Skill Forge help users create?
faq.answer: It helps users create and improve AI assistant skills with clear purpose, structure, resources, validation, and packaging steps.

faq.question: Does this skill generate production-ready skills automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured process, but users still need to review behavior, safety, and quality.

faq.question: Can it improve existing skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for diagnosing weak metadata, unclear instructions, missing resources, and validation gaps.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Python scripts that validate skill structure and package a skill directory into a zip file.

faq.question: Why does the audit show warnings?
faq.answer: The skill documents external commands and includes scripts that read files and create archives, which require user review.

faq.question: Which tools is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: The metadata indicates support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
